NKJV.1: Now when they drew near Jerusalem, and came to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
NKJV.2: saying to them, “Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Loose them and bring them to Me.
NKJV.3: And if anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord has need of them,’ and immediately he will send them.”
NKJV.4: All this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying:
NKJV.5: “Tell  the daughter of Zion, ‘Behold, your King is coming to you, Lowly, and sitting on a donkey, A colt, the foal of a donkey.’ ”
NKJV.6: So the disciples went and did as Jesus commanded them.
NKJV.7: They brought the donkey and the colt, laid their clothes on them, and set Him on them.
NKJV.8: And a very great multitude spread their clothes on the road; others cut down branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NKJV.9: Then the multitudes who went before and those who followed cried out, saying: “Hosanna to the Son of David! ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the LORD!’ Hosanna in the highest!”
NKJV.10: And when He had come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, “Who is this?”
NKJV.11: So the multitudes said, “This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth of Galilee.”
NKJV.12: Then Jesus went into the temple of God and drove out all those who bought and sold in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
NKJV.13: And He said to them, “It is written,  ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a  ‘den of thieves.’ ”
NKJV.14: Then the blind and the lame came to Him in the temple, and He healed them.
NKJV.15: But when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that He did, and the children crying out in the temple and saying, “Hosanna to the Son of David!” they were indignant
NKJV.16: and said to Him, “Do You hear what these are saying?” And Jesus said to them, “Yes. Have you never read,  ‘Out of the mouth of babes and nursing infants You have perfected praise’?”
NKJV.17: Then He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and He lodged there.
NKJV.18: Now in the morning, as He returned to the city, He was hungry.
NKJV.19: And seeing a fig tree by the road, He came to it and found nothing on it but leaves, and said to it, “Let no fruit grow on you ever again.” Immediately the fig tree withered away.
NKJV.20: And when the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree wither away so soon?”
NKJV.21: So Jesus answered and said to them, “Assuredly, I say to you,  if you have faith and  do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree,  but also if you say to this mountain, ‘Be removed and be cast into the sea,’ it will be done.
NKJV.22: And  whatever things you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.”
NKJV.23: Now when He came into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people confronted Him as He was teaching, and said, “By what authority are You doing these things? And who gave You this authority?”
NKJV.24: But Jesus answered and said to them, “I also will ask you one thing, which if you tell Me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things:
NKJV.25: The  baptism of  John—where was it from? From heaven or from men?” And they reasoned among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
NKJV.26: But if we say, ‘From men,’ we fear the multitude, for all count John as a prophet.”
NKJV.27: So they answered Jesus and said, “We do not know.” And He said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
NKJV.28: “But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first and said, ‘Son, go, work today in my  vineyard.’
NKJV.29: He answered and said, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he regretted it and went.
NKJV.30: Then he came to the second and said likewise. And he answered and said, ‘I go, sir,’ but he did not go.
NKJV.31: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said to Him, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Assuredly, I say to you that tax collectors and harlots enter the kingdom of God before you.
NKJV.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him; but tax collectors and harlots believed him; and when you saw it, you did not afterward relent and believe him.
NKJV.33: “Hear another parable: There was a certain landowner  who planted a vineyard and set a hedge around it, dug a winepress in it and built a tower. And he leased it to vinedressers and  went into a far country.
NKJV.34: Now when vintage-time drew near, he sent his servants to the vinedressers, that they might receive its fruit.
NKJV.35: And the vinedressers took his servants, beat one, killed one, and stoned another.
NKJV.36: Again he sent other servants, more than the first, and they did likewise to them.
NKJV.37: Then last of all he sent his  son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NKJV.38: But when the vinedressers saw the son, they said among themselves,  ‘This is the heir.  Come, let us kill him and seize his inheritance.’
NKJV.39: So they took him and cast him out of the vineyard and killed him.
NKJV.40: “Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those vinedressers?”
NKJV.41: They said to Him, “He will destroy those wicked men miserably, and lease his vineyard to other vinedressers who will render to him the fruits in their seasons.”
NKJV.42: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures: ‘The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief cornerstone. This was the LORD’s doing, And it is marvelous in our eyes’?
NKJV.43: “Therefore I say to you,  the kingdom of God will be taken from you and given to a nation bearing the fruits of it.
NKJV.44: And  whoever falls on this stone will be broken; but on whomever it falls,  it will grind him to powder.”
NKJV.45: Now when the chief priests and Pharisees heard His parables, they perceived that He was speaking of them.
NKJV.46: But when they sought to lay hands on Him, they feared the multitudes, because they took Him for a prophet.

ESV.1: Now when they drew near to Jerusalem and came to Bethphage, to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
ESV.2: saying to them, “Go into the village in front of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Untie them and bring them to me.
ESV.3: If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and he will send them at once.”
ESV.4: This took place to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet, saying,
ESV.5: “Say to the daughter of Zion, ‘Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, on a colt,  the foal of a beast of burden.’”
ESV.6: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them.
ESV.7: They brought the donkey and the colt and put on them their cloaks, and he sat on them.
ESV.8: Most of the crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
ESV.9: And the crowds that went before him and that followed him were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
ESV.10: And when he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”
ESV.11: And the crowds said, “This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.”
ESV.12: And Jesus entered the temple  and drove out all who sold and bought in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons.
ESV.13: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you make it a den of robbers.”
ESV.14: And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
ESV.15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying out in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David!” they were indignant,
ESV.16: and they said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” And Jesus said to them, “Yes; have you never read,  “‘Out of the mouth of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise’?”
ESV.17: And leaving them, he went out of the city to Bethany and lodged there.
ESV.18: In the morning, as he was returning to the city, he became hungry.
ESV.19: And seeing a fig tree by the wayside, he went to it and found nothing on it but only leaves. And he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” And the fig tree withered at once.
ESV.20: When the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree wither at once?”
ESV.21: And Jesus answered them,  “Truly, I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ it will happen.
ESV.22: And whatever you ask in prayer, you will receive, if you have faith.”
ESV.23: And when he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came up to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
ESV.24: Jesus answered them, “I also will ask you one question, and if you tell me the answer, then I also will tell you by what authority I do these things.
ESV.25: The baptism of John, from where did it come? From heaven or from man?” And they discussed it among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
ESV.26: But if we say, ‘From man,’ we are afraid of the crowd, for they all hold that John was a prophet.”
ESV.27: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
ESV.28: “What do you think? A man had two sons. And he went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
ESV.29: And he answered, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he changed his mind and went.
ESV.30: And he went to the other son and said the same. And he answered, ‘I go, sir,’ but did not go.
ESV.31: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly, I say to you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes go into the kingdom of God before you.
ESV.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him. And even when you saw it, you did not afterward change your minds and believe him.
ESV.33: “Hear another parable. There was a master of a house who planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a winepress in it and built a tower and leased it to tenants, and went into another country.
ESV.34: When the season for fruit drew near, he sent his servants  to the tenants to get his fruit.
ESV.35: And the tenants took his servants and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
ESV.36: Again he sent other servants, more than the first. And they did the same to them.
ESV.37: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
ESV.38: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and have his inheritance.’
ESV.39: And they took him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
ESV.40: When therefore the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
ESV.41: They said to him, “He will put those wretches to a miserable death and let out the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the fruits in their seasons.”
ESV.42: Jesus said to them,  “Have you never read in the Scriptures:  “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;  this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?
ESV.43: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people producing its fruits.
ESV.44: And the one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and when it falls on anyone, it will crush him.”
ESV.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they perceived that he was speaking about them.
ESV.46: And although they were seeking to arrest him, they feared the crowds, because they held him to be a prophet.

NASB.1: When they had approached Jerusalem and had come to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
NASB.2: saying to them, 'Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied [there] and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to Me.
NASB.3: 'If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, 'The Lord has need of them,' and immediately he will send them.'
NASB.4: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:
NASB.5: 'SAY TO THE DAUGHTER OF ZION, 'BEHOLD YOUR KING IS COMING TO YOU, GENTLE, AND MOUNTED ON A DONKEY, EVEN ON A COLT, THE FOAL OF A BEAST OF BURDEN.''
NASB.6: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had instructed them,
NASB.7: and brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their coats on them; and He sat on the coats.
NASB.8: Most of the crowd spread their coats in the road, and others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading them in the road.
NASB.9: The crowds going ahead of Him, and those who followed, were shouting, 'Hosanna to the Son of David; BLESSED IS HE WHO COMES IN THE NAME OF THE LORD; Hosanna in the highest!'
NASB.10: When He had entered Jerusalem, all the city was stirred, saying, 'Who is this?'
NASB.11: And the crowds were saying, 'This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth in Galilee.'
NASB.12: And Jesus entered the temple and drove out all those who were buying and selling in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
NASB.13: And He said to them, 'It is written, 'MY HOUSE SHALL BE CALLED A HOUSE OF PRAYER'; but you are making it a ROBBERS' DEN.'
NASB.14: And [the] blind and [the] lame came to Him in the temple, and He healed them.
NASB.15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that He had done, and the children who were shouting in the temple, 'Hosanna to the Son of David,' they became indignant
NASB.16: and said to Him, 'Do You hear what these [children] are saying?' And Jesus said to them, 'Yes; have you never read, 'OUT OF THE MOUTH OF INFANTS AND NURSING BABIES YOU HAVE PREPARED PRAISE FOR YOURSELF'?'
NASB.17: And He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
NASB.18: Now in the morning, when He was returning to the city, He became hungry.
NASB.19: Seeing a lone fig tree by the road, He came to it and found nothing on it except leaves only; and He said to it, 'No longer shall there ever be [any] fruit from you.' And at once the fig tree withered.
NASB.20: Seeing [this], the disciples were amazed and asked, 'How did the fig tree wither [all] at once?'
NASB.21: And Jesus answered and said to them, 'Truly I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' it will happen.
NASB.22: 'And all things you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.'
NASB.23: When He entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to Him while He was teaching, and said, 'By what authority are You doing these things, and who gave You this authority?'
NASB.24: Jesus said to them, 'I will also ask you one thing, which if you tell Me, I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.
NASB.25: 'The baptism of John was from what [source], from heaven or from men?' And they [began] reasoning among themselves, saying, 'If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say to us, 'Then why did you not believe him?'
NASB.26: 'But if we say, 'From men,' we fear the people; for they all regard John as a prophet.'
NASB.27: And answering Jesus, they said, 'We do not know.' He also said to them, 'Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
NASB.28: 'But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first and said, 'Son, go work today in the vineyard.'
NASB.29: 'And he answered, 'I will not'; but afterward he regretted it and went.
NASB.30: 'The man came to the second and said the same thing; and he answered, 'I [will], sir'; but he did not go.
NASB.31: 'Which of the two did the will of his father?' They said, 'The first.' Jesus said to them, 'Truly I say to you that the tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you.
NASB.32: 'For John came to you in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him; but the tax collectors and prostitutes did believe him; and you, seeing [this], did not even feel remorse afterward so as to believe him.
NASB.33: 'Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who PLANTED A VINEYARD AND PUT A WALL AROUND IT AND DUG A WINE PRESS IN IT, AND BUILT A TOWER, and rented it out to vine-growers and went on a journey.
NASB.34: 'When the harvest time approached, he sent his slaves to the vine-growers to receive his produce.
NASB.35: 'The vine-growers took his slaves and beat one, and killed another, and stoned a third.
NASB.36: 'Again he sent another group of slaves larger than the first; and they did the same thing to them.
NASB.37: 'But afterward he sent his son to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
NASB.38: 'But when the vine-growers saw the son, they said among themselves, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him and seize his inheritance.'
NASB.39: 'They took him, and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
NASB.40: 'Therefore when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those vine-growers?'
NASB.41: They said to Him, 'He will bring those wretches to a wretched end, and will rent out the vineyard to other vine-growers who will pay him the proceeds at the [proper] seasons.'
NASB.42: Jesus said to them, 'Did you never read in the Scriptures, 'THE STONE WHICH THE BUILDERS REJECTED, THIS BECAME THE CHIEF CORNER [stone]; THIS CAME ABOUT FROM THE LORD, AND IT IS MARVELOUS IN OUR EYES '?
NASB.43: 'Therefore I say to you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people, producing the fruit of it.
NASB.44: 'And he who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; but on whomever it falls, it will scatter him like dust.'
NASB.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard His parables, they understood that He was speaking about them.
NASB.46: When they sought to seize Him, they feared the people, because they considered Him to be a prophet.

NLT.1: As Jesus and the disciples approached Jerusalem, they came to the town of Bethphage on the Mount of Olives. Jesus sent two of them on ahead.
NLT.2: “Go into the village over there,” he said. “As soon as you enter it, you will see a donkey tied there, with its colt beside it. Untie them and bring them to me.
NLT.3: If anyone asks what you are doing, just say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and he will immediately let you take them.”
NLT.4: This took place to fulfill the prophecy that said,
NLT.5:
“Tell the people of Jerusalem,
‘Look, your King is coming to you.
He is humble, riding on a donkey —
riding on a donkey’s colt.’”
NLT.6: The two disciples did as Jesus commanded.
NLT.7: They brought the donkey and the colt to him and threw their garments over the colt, and he sat on it.
NLT.8: Most of the crowd spread their garments on the road ahead of him, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NLT.9:
Jesus was in the center of the procession, and the people all around him were shouting, “Praise God for the Son of David!
Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the LORD!
Praise God in highest heaven!”
NLT.10: The entire city of Jerusalem was in an uproar as he entered. “Who is this?” they asked.
NLT.11: And the crowds replied, “It’s Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.”
NLT.12: Jesus entered the Temple and began to drive out all the people buying and selling animals for sacrifice. He knocked over the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves.
NLT.13: He said to them, “The Scriptures declare, ‘My Temple will be called a house of prayer,’ but you have turned it into a den of thieves!”
NLT.14: The blind and the lame came to him in the Temple, and he healed them.
NLT.15: The leading priests and the teachers of religious law saw these wonderful miracles and heard even the children in the Temple shouting, “Praise God for the Son of David.” But the leaders were indignant.
NLT.16: They asked Jesus, “Do you hear what these children are saying?” “Yes,” Jesus replied. “Haven’t you ever read the Scriptures? For they say, ‘You have taught children and infants to give you praise.’ ”
NLT.17: Then he returned to Bethany, where he stayed overnight.
NLT.18: In the morning, as Jesus was returning to Jerusalem, he was hungry,
NLT.19: and he noticed a fig tree beside the road. He went over to see if there were any figs, but there were only leaves. Then he said to it, “May you never bear fruit again!” And immediately the fig tree withered up.
NLT.20: The disciples were amazed when they saw this and asked, “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?”
NLT.21: Then Jesus told them, “I tell you the truth, if you have faith and don’t doubt, you can do things like this and much more. You can even say to this mountain, ‘May you be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and it will happen.
NLT.22: You can pray for anything, and if you have faith, you will receive it.”
NLT.23: When Jesus returned to the Temple and began teaching, the leading priests and elders came up to him. They demanded, “By what authority are you doing all these things? Who gave you the right?”
NLT.24: “I’ll tell you by what authority I do these things if you answer one question,” Jesus replied.
NLT.25: “Did John’s authority to baptize come from heaven, or was it merely human?” They talked it over among themselves. “If we say it was from heaven, he will ask us why we didn’t believe John.
NLT.26: But if we say it was merely human, we’ll be mobbed because the people believe John was a prophet.”
NLT.27: So they finally replied, “We don’t know.” And Jesus responded, “Then I won’t tell you by what authority I do these things.
NLT.28: “But what do you think about this? A man with two sons told the older boy, ‘Son, go out and work in the vineyard today.’
NLT.29: The son answered, ‘No, I won’t go,’ but later he changed his mind and went anyway.
NLT.30: Then the father told the other son, ‘You go,’ and he said, ‘Yes, sir, I will.’ But he didn’t go.
NLT.31: “Which of the two obeyed his father?” They replied, “The first.” Then Jesus explained his meaning: “I tell you the truth, corrupt tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the Kingdom of God before you do.
NLT.32: For John the Baptist came and showed you the right way to live, but you didn’t believe him, while tax collectors and prostitutes did. And even when you saw this happening, you refused to believe him and repent of your sins.
NLT.33: “Now listen to another story. A certain landowner planted a vineyard, built a wall around it, dug a pit for pressing out the grape juice, and built a lookout tower. Then he leased the vineyard to tenant farmers and moved to another country.
NLT.34: At the time of the grape harvest, he sent his servants to collect his share of the crop.
NLT.35: But the farmers grabbed his servants, beat one, killed one, and stoned another.
NLT.36: So the landowner sent a larger group of his servants to collect for him, but the results were the same.
NLT.37: “Finally, the owner sent his son, thinking, ‘Surely they will respect my son.’
NLT.38: “But when the tenant farmers saw his son coming, they said to one another, ‘Here comes the heir to this estate. Come on, let’s kill him and get the estate for ourselves!’
NLT.39: So they grabbed him, dragged him out of the vineyard, and murdered him.
NLT.40: “When the owner of the vineyard returns,” Jesus asked, “what do you think he will do to those farmers?”
NLT.41: The religious leaders replied, “He will put the wicked men to a horrible death and lease the vineyard to others who will give him his share of the crop after each harvest.”
NLT.42:
Then Jesus asked them, “Didn’t you ever read this in the Scriptures? ‘The stone that the builders rejected
has now become the cornerstone.
This is the LORD’s doing,
and it is wonderful to see.’
NLT.43: I tell you, the Kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a nation that will produce the proper fruit.
NLT.44: Anyone who stumbles over that stone will be broken to pieces, and it will crush anyone it falls on. ”
NLT.45: When the leading priests and Pharisees heard this parable, they realized he was telling the story against them — they were the wicked farmers.
NLT.46: They wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowds, who considered Jesus to be a prophet.

WEB.1: When they came near to Jerusalem, and came to Bethsphage,  to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
WEB.2: saying to them, “Go into the village that is opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Untie them, and bring them to me.
WEB.3: If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and immediately he will send them.”
WEB.4: All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying,
WEB.5: “Tell the daughter of Zion, behold, your King comes to you, humble, and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
WEB.6: The disciples went, and did just as Jesus commanded them,
WEB.7: and brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their clothes on them; and he sat on them.
WEB.8: A very great multitude spread their clothes on the road. Others cut branches from the trees, and spread them on the road.
WEB.9: The multitudes who went in front of him, and those who followed, kept shouting, “Hosanna  to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
WEB.10: When he had come into Jerusalem, all the city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”
WEB.11: The multitudes said, “This is the prophet, Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.”
WEB.12: Jesus entered into the temple of God, and drove out all of those who sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables and the seats of those who sold the doves.
WEB.13: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’  but you have made it a den of robbers!”
WEB.14: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
WEB.15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children who were crying in the temple and saying, “Hosanna to the son of David!” they were indignant,
WEB.16: and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes. Did you never read, ‘Out of the mouth of babes and nursing babies you have perfected praise?’”
WEB.17: He left them, and went out of the city to Bethany, and camped there.
WEB.18: Now in the morning, as he returned to the city, he was hungry.
WEB.19: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he came to it, and found nothing on it but leaves. He said to it, “Let there be no fruit from you forever!”  Immediately the fig tree withered away.
WEB.20: When the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree immediately wither away?”
WEB.21: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, if you have faith, and don’t doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you told this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ it would be done.
WEB.22: All things, whatever you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.”
WEB.23: When he had come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority do you do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
WEB.24: Jesus answered them, “I also will ask you one question, which if you tell me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things.
WEB.25: The baptism of John, where was it from? From heaven or from men?”  They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
WEB.26: But if we say, ‘From men,’ we fear the multitude, for all hold John as a prophet.”
WEB.27: They answered Jesus, and said, “We don’t know.” He also said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
WEB.28: But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first, and said, ‘Son, go work today in my vineyard.’
WEB.29: He answered, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he changed his mind, and went.
WEB.30: He came to the second, and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I go, sir,’ but he didn’t go.
WEB.31: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said to him, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom before you.
WEB.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you didn’t believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him. When you saw it, you didn’t even repent afterward, that you might believe him.
WEB.33: “Hear another parable. There was a man who was a master of a household, who planted a vineyard, set a hedge about it, dug a wine press in it, built a tower, leased it out to farmers, and went into another country.
WEB.34: When the season for the fruit came near, he sent his servants to the farmers, to receive his fruit.
WEB.35: The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
WEB.36: Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they treated them the same way.
WEB.37: But afterward he sent to them his son, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
WEB.38: But the farmers, when they saw the son, said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and seize his inheritance.’
WEB.39: So they took him, and threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
WEB.40: When therefore the lord of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?”
WEB.41: They told him, “He will miserably destroy those miserable men, and will lease out the vineyard to other farmers, who will give him the fruit in its season.”
WEB.42: Jesus said to them, “Did you never read in the Scriptures,   ‘The stone which the builders rejected,  the same was made the head of the corner.  This was from the Lord.  It is marvelous in our eyes?’
WEB.43: “Therefore I tell you, God’s Kingdom will be taken away from you, and will be given to a nation producing its fruit.
WEB.44: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but on whomever it will fall, it will scatter him as dust.”
WEB.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they perceived that he spoke about them.
WEB.46: When they sought to seize him, they feared the multitudes, because they considered him to be a prophet.

CSB17.1: When they approached Jerusalem <sup>ⓘ</sup> and came to Bethphage at the Mount of Olives, <sup>ⓙ</sup> Jesus then sent two disciples,
CSB17.2: telling them, “Go into the village ahead of you. At once you will find a donkey tied there with her foal. Untie them and bring them to me.
CSB17.3: If anyone says anything to you, say that the Lord needs them, and he will send them at once.”
CSB17.4: This took place so that what was spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled:
CSB17.5:
Tell Daughter Zion,
“See, your King is coming to you,
gentle, and mounted on a donkey,
and on a colt,
the foal of a donkey.” [1] [2]
CSB17.6: The disciples went and did just as Jesus directed them.
CSB17.7: They brought the donkey and its foal; then they laid their clothes on them, and he sat on them.
CSB17.8: A very large crowd spread their clothes on the road; others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading them on the road.
CSB17.9:
Then the crowds who went ahead of him and those who followed shouted: Hosanna to the Son of David!
Blessed is he who comes in the name
of the Lord! [3]
Hosanna in the highest heaven! ⓚ
CSB17.10: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in an uproar, saying, “Who is this?”
CSB17.11: The crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus <sup>ⓛ</sup> from Nazareth in Galilee.”
CSB17.12: Jesus went into the temple <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> and threw out all those buying and selling. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: He said to them, “It is written, my house will be called a house of prayer, ⓐbut you are making it a den of thieves!” [5] ⓑ
CSB17.14: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
CSB17.15: When the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonders that he did and the children shouting in the temple,” Hosanna to the Son of David!” they were indignant
CSB17.16:
and said to him, “Do you hear what these children are saying?” Jesus replied, “Yes, have you never read: You have prepared [6] praise ⓒ
from the mouths of infants and nursing babies?” [7]
CSB17.17: Then he left them, went out of the city to Bethany, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and spent the night there.
CSB17.18: Early in the morning, <sup>ⓔ</sup> as he was returning to the city, he was hungry.
CSB17.19: Seeing a lone fig tree by the road, he went up to it and found nothing on it except leaves. And he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” At once the fig tree withered.
CSB17.20: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed and said, “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?”
CSB17.21: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you tell this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.22: And if you believe, you will receive <sup>ⓖ</sup>whatever you ask for in prayer.”
CSB17.23: When he entered the temple, <sup>ⓗ</sup> the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority?” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.24: Jesus answered them, “I will also ask you one question, and if you answer it for me, then I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
CSB17.25: Did John’s baptism come from heaven, or was it of human origin?” They discussed it among themselves, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Then why didn’t you believe him? ’ <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.26: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we’re afraid of the crowd, <sup>ⓚ</sup> because everyone considers John to be a prophet.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.27: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
CSB17.28: “What do you think? A man had two sons. He went to the first and said, ‘My son, go work in the vineyard today.’ <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.29: “He answered, ‘I don’t want to’ but later he changed his mind and went.
CSB17.30: Then the man went to the other and said the same thing. ‘I will, sir,’ he answered, but he didn’t go.
CSB17.31: Which of the two did his father’s will?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God before you.
CSB17.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, <sup>ⓝ</sup>and you didn’t believe him. Tax collectors and prostitutes did believe him; but you, when you saw it, didn’t even change your minds then and believe him.
CSB17.33: “Listen <sup>ⓐ</sup> to another parable: <sup>ⓑ</sup> There was a landowner, who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a watchtower. <sup>ⓒ</sup> He leased it to tenant farmers and went away. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.34: When the time came to harvest fruit, he sent his servants to the farmers to collect his fruit.
CSB17.35: The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned a third. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.36: Again, he sent other servants, more than the first group, and they did the same to them.
CSB17.37: Finally, he sent his son to them. ‘They will respect my son,’ he said.
CSB17.38: “But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him and take his inheritance.’ <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.39: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
CSB17.40: Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?”
CSB17.41: “He will completely destroy those terrible men,” they told him, “and lease his vineyard to other farmers who will give him his fruit at the harvest.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.42:
Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone that the builders rejected
has become the cornerstone. [8]
This is what the Lord has done
and it is wonderful in our eyes? [9] ⓗ
CSB17.43: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people producing its fruit.
CSB17.44: Whoever falls on this stone will be broken to pieces;  <sup>ⓘ</sup>but on whomever it falls, it will shatter him.” <sup>[10]</sup>
CSB17.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, <sup>ⓙ</sup> they knew he was speaking about them.
CSB17.46: Although they were looking for a way to arrest him, they feared the crowds, because the people regarded him as a prophet. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: When they approached Jerusalem and had reached Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples [ahead],
AMP.2: saying to them, "Go into the village opposite you, and at once you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to Me.
AMP.3: If anyone says anything to you, you should say, 'The Lord needs them,' and without delay the owner will send them [with you]."
AMP.4: This happened so that what was spoken by the prophet would be fulfilled, saying:
AMP.5:
"Tell the daughter of Zion (the people of Jerusalem),
'Behold, your King is coming to you,
Gentle and mounted on a donkey,
Even on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.'"
AMP.6: Then the disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed them,
AMP.7: and they brought the donkey and the colt, and placed their coats on them; and Jesus sat on the coats.
AMP.8: Most of the crowd spread their coats on the road [as before a king], while others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading them on the road.
AMP.9:
The crowds that went ahead of Him, and those that followed Him, were shouting [in praise and adoration],
" Hosanna to the Son of David (Messiah);
Blessed [praised, glorified] is He who comes in the name of the Lord;
Hosanna in the highest [heaven]!"
AMP.10: When He entered Jerusalem, all the city was trembling [with excitement], saying, "Who is this?"
AMP.11: And the crowds were saying, "This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth in Galilee."
AMP.12: And Jesus entered the temple [grounds] and drove out [with force] all who were buying and selling [birds and animals for sacrifice] in the temple area, and He turned over the tables of the moneychangers [who made a profit exchanging foreign money for temple coinage] and the chairs of those who were selling doves [for sacrifice].
AMP.13: Jesus said to them, "It is written [in Scripture], 'My house shall be called a house of prayer'; but you are making it a robbers' den."
AMP.14: And the blind and the lame came to Him in [the porticoes and courts of] the temple area, and He healed them.
AMP.15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful and miraculous things that Jesus had done, and heard the boys who were shouting in [the porticoes and courts of] the temple [in praise and adoration], "Hosanna to the Son of David (the Messiah)," they became indignant
AMP.16: and they said to Him, "Do You hear what these children are saying?" And Jesus replied to them, "Yes; have you never read [in the Scripture], 'Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babies You have prepared and provided praise for Yourself'?"
AMP.17: Then He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
AMP.18: Now early in the morning, as Jesus was coming back to the city, He was hungry.
AMP.19: Seeing a lone fig tree at the roadside, He went to it and found nothing but leaves on it; and He said to it, "Never again will fruit come from you." And at once the fig tree withered.
AMP.20: When the disciples saw it, they were astonished and asked, "How is it that the fig tree has withered away all at once?"
AMP.21: Jesus replied to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, if you have faith [personal trust and confidence in Me] and do not doubt or allow yourself to be drawn in two directions, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be taken up and thrown into the sea,' it will happen [if God wills it].
AMP.22: And whatever you ask for in prayer, believing, you will receive."
AMP.23: When He entered the temple area, the chief priests and elders of the people came to Him as He was teaching and said, "By what [kind of] authority are You doing these things, and who gave You this authority [to exercise this power]?"
AMP.24: Jesus replied to them, "I will also ask you a question, and if you tell Me the answer, then I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
AMP.25: The baptism of John—from where did it come? From heaven [that is, ordained by God] or from men?" And they began debating among themselves [considering the implications of their answer], saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say to us, 'Then why did you not believe John?'
AMP.26: But if we say, 'From men,' we are afraid of the [response of the] crowd; for they all regard John as a prophet."
AMP.27: So they answered Jesus, "We do not know." And He said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what [kind of] authority I do these things."
AMP.28: "What do you think? There was a man who had two sons, and he came to the first and said, 'Son, go and work in the vineyard today.'
AMP.29: And he answered, 'I will not'; but afterward he regretted it and changed his mind and went.
AMP.30: Then the man came to the second son and said the same thing; and he replied, 'I will, sir'; but he did not go.
AMP.31: Which of the two did the will of the father?" The chief priests and elders replied, "The first one." Jesus said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you.
AMP.32: For John came to you [walking] in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him; but the tax collectors and the prostitutes did believe him; and you, seeing this, did not even change your mind afterward and believe him [accepting what he proclaimed to you].
AMP.33: "Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard and put a wall around it and dug a wine press in it, and built a tower, and rented it out to tenant farmers and went on a journey [to another country].
AMP.34: When the harvest time approached, he sent his servants to the tenants to get his [share of the] fruit.
AMP.35: But the tenants took his servants and beat one, and killed another, and stoned a third.
AMP.36: Again he sent other servants, more than the first time; and they treated them the same way.
AMP.37: Finally he sent his own son to them, saying, 'They will respect my son and have regard for him.'
AMP.38: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, 'This [man] is the heir; come on, let us kill him and seize his inheritance.'
AMP.39: So they took the son and threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
AMP.40: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes back, what will he do to those tenants?"
AMP.41: They said to Him, "He will put those despicable men to a miserable end, and rent out the vineyard to other tenants [of good character] who will pay him the proceeds at the proper seasons."
AMP.42:
Jesus asked them, "Have you never read in the Scriptures:
'The [very]Stone which the builders rejected and threw away,
Has become the chief Cornerstone;
This is the Lord's doing,
And it is marvelous and wonderful in our eyes'?
AMP.43: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to [another] people who will produce the fruit of it.
AMP.44: And he who falls on this Stone will be broken to pieces; but he on whom it falls will be crushed."
AMP.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard His parables, they understood that He was talking about them.
AMP.46: And although they were trying to arrest Him, they feared the people, because they regarded Jesus as a prophet.

MSG.1: When they neared Jerusalem, having arrived at Bethphage on Mount Olives, Jesus sent two disciples
MSG.2: with these instructions: "Go over to the village across from you. You'll find a donkey tethered there, her colt with her. Untie her and bring them to me.
MSG.3: If anyone asks what you're doing, say, 'The Master needs them!' He will send them with you."
MSG.4: This is the full story of what was sketched earlier by the prophet:
MSG.5: Tell Zion's daughter, "Look, your king's on his way, poised and ready, mounted On a donkey, on a colt, foal of a pack animal."
MSG.6: The disciples went and did exactly what Jesus told them to do.
MSG.7: They led the donkey and colt out, laid some of their clothes on them, and Jesus mounted.
MSG.8: Nearly all the people in the crowd threw their garments down on the road, giving him a royal welcome. Others cut branches from the trees and threw them down as a welcome mat.
MSG.9: Crowds went ahead and crowds followed, all of them calling out, "Hosanna to David's son!" "Blessed is he who comes in God's name!" "Hosanna in highest heaven!"
MSG.10: As he made his entrance into Jerusalem, the whole city was shaken. Unnerved, people were asking, "What's going on here? Who is this?"
MSG.11: The parade crowd answered, "This is the prophet Jesus, the one from Nazareth in Galilee."
MSG.12: Jesus went straight to the Temple and threw out everyone who had set up shop, buying and selling. He kicked over the tables of loan sharks and the stalls of dove merchants.
MSG.13: He quoted this text: My house was designated a house of prayer; You have made it a hangout for thieves.
MSG.14: Now there was room for the blind and crippled to get in. They came to Jesus and he healed them.
MSG.15: When the religious leaders saw the outrageous things he was doing, and heard all the children running and shouting through the Temple, "Hosanna to David's Son!" they were up in arms and took him to task.
MSG.16: "Do you hear what these children are saying?" Jesus said, "Yes, I hear them. And haven't you read in God's Word, 'From the mouths of children and babies I'll furnish a place of praise'?"
MSG.17: Fed up, Jesus turned on his heel and left the city for Bethany, where he spent the night.
MSG.18: Early the next morning Jesus was returning to the city. He was hungry.
MSG.19: Seeing a lone fig tree alongside the road, he approached it anticipating a breakfast of figs. When he got to the tree, there was nothing but fig leaves. He said, "No more figs from this tree—ever!" The fig tree withered on the spot, a dry stick.
MSG.20: The disciples saw it happen. They rubbed their eyes, saying, "Did we really see this? A leafy tree one minute, a dry stick the next?"
MSG.21: But Jesus was matter-of-fact: "Yes—and if you embrace this kingdom life and don't doubt God, you'll not only do minor feats like I did to the fig tree, but also triumph over huge obstacles. This mountain, for instance, you'll tell, 'Go jump in the lake,' and it will jump.
MSG.22: Absolutely everything, ranging from small to large, as you make it a part of your believing prayer, gets included as you lay hold of God."
MSG.23: Then he was back in the Temple, teaching. The high priests and leaders of the people came up and demanded, "Show us your credentials. Who authorized you to teach here?"
MSG.24: Jesus responded, "First let me ask you a question. You answer my question and I'll answer yours.
MSG.25: About the baptism of John—who authorized it: heaven or humans?" They were on the spot and knew it. They pulled back into a huddle and whispered, "If we say 'heaven,' he'll ask us why we didn't believe him;
MSG.26: if we say 'humans,' we're up against it with the people because they all hold John up as a prophet."
MSG.27: They decided to concede that round to Jesus. "We don't know," they answered. Jesus said, "Then neither will I answer your question.
MSG.28: "Tell me what you think of this story: A man had two sons. He went up to the first and said, 'Son, go out for the day and work in the vineyard.'
MSG.29: "The son answered, 'I don't want to.' Later on he thought better of it and went.
MSG.30: "The father gave the same command to the second son. He answered, 'Sure, glad to.' But he never went.
MSG.31: "Which of the two sons did what the father asked?" They said, "The first." Jesus said, "Yes, and I tell you that crooks and whores are going to precede you into God's kingdom.
MSG.32: John came to you showing you the right road. You turned up your noses at him, but the crooks and whores believed him. Even when you saw their changed lives, you didn't care enough to change and believe him.
MSG.33: "Here's another story. Listen closely. There was once a man, a wealthy farmer, who planted a vineyard. He fenced it, dug a winepress, put up a watchtower, then turned it over to the farmhands and went off on a trip.
MSG.34: When it was time to harvest the grapes, he sent his servants back to collect his profits.
MSG.35: "The farmhands grabbed the first servant and beat him up. The next one they murdered. They threw stones at the third but he got away.
MSG.36: The owner tried again, sending more servants. They got the same treatment.
MSG.37: The owner was at the end of his rope. He decided to send his son. 'Surely,' he thought, 'they will respect my son.'
MSG.38: "But when the farmhands saw the son arrive, they rubbed their hands in greed. 'This is the heir! Let's kill him and have it all for ourselves.'
MSG.39: They grabbed him, threw him out, and killed him.
MSG.40: "Now, when the owner of the vineyard arrives home from his trip, what do you think he will do to the farmhands?"
MSG.41: "He'll kill them—a rotten bunch, and good riddance," they answered. "Then he'll assign the vineyard to farmhands who will hand over the profits when it's time."
MSG.42: Jesus said, "Right—and you can read it for yourselves in your Bibles: The stone the masons threw out is now the cornerstone. This is God's work; we rub our eyes, we can hardly believe it!
MSG.43: "This is the way it is with you. God's kingdom will be taken back from you and handed over to a people who will live out a kingdom life.
MSG.44: Whoever stumbles on this Stone gets shattered; whoever the Stone falls on gets smashed."
MSG.45: When the religious leaders heard this story, they knew it was aimed at them.
MSG.46: They wanted to arrest Jesus and put him in jail, but, intimidated by public opinion, they held back. Most people held him to be a prophet of God.

NIV.1:
The Triumphal Entry
As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
NIV.2: saying to them, “Go to the village ahead of you, and at once you will find a donkey tied there, with her colt by her. Untie them and bring them to me.
NIV.3: If anyone says anything to you, tell him that the Lord needs them, and he will send them right away.”
NIV.4: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:
NIV.5:
“Say to the Daughter of Zion,
‘See, your king comes to you,
gentle and riding on a donkey,
on a colt, the foal of a donkey.’”
NIV.6: The disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed them.
NIV.7: They brought the donkey and the colt, placed their cloaks on them, and Jesus sat on them.
NIV.8: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NIV.9:
The crowds that went ahead of him and those that followed shouted,
“Hosanna to the Son of David!”
“Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!”
“Hosanna in the highest!”
NIV.10: When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, “Who is this?”
NIV.11: The crowds answered, “This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.”
NIV.12:
Jesus at the Temple
Jesus entered the temple area and drove out all who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the benches of those selling doves.
NIV.13: “It is written,” he said to them, “‘My house will be called a house of prayer,’ but you are making it a ‘den of robbers.’”
NIV.14: The blind and the lame came to him at the temple, and he healed them.
NIV.15: But when the chief priests and the teachers of the law saw the wonderful things he did and the children shouting in the temple area, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they were indignant.
NIV.16:
“Do you hear what these children are saying?” they asked him.
“Yes,” replied Jesus, “have you never read,
“‘From the lips of children and infants
you have ordained praise’?”
NIV.17: And he left them and went out of the city to Bethany, where he spent the night.
NIV.18:
The Fig Tree Withers
Early in the morning, as he was on his way back to the city, he was hungry.
NIV.19: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he went up to it but found nothing on it except leaves. Then he said to it, “May you never bear fruit again!” Immediately the tree withered.
NIV.20: When the disciples saw this, they were amazed. “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?” they asked.
NIV.21: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only can you do what was done to the fig tree, but also you can say to this mountain, ‘Go, throw yourself into the sea,’ and it will be done.
NIV.22: If you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer.”
NIV.23:
The Authority of Jesus Questioned
Jesus entered the temple courts, and, while he was teaching, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him. “By what authority are you doing these things?” they asked. “And who gave you this authority?”
NIV.24: Jesus replied, “I will also ask you one question. If you answer me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
NIV.25:
John’s baptism—where did it come from? Was it from heaven, or from men?”
They discussed it among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask, ‘Then why didn’t you believe him?’
NIV.26: But if we say, ‘From men’—we are afraid of the people, for they all hold that John was a prophet.”
NIV.27:
So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.”
Then he said, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
NIV.28:
The Parable of the Two Sons
“What do you think? There was a man who had two sons. He went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work today in the vineyard.’
NIV.29: “‘I will not,’ he answered, but later he changed his mind and went.
NIV.30: “Then the father went to the other son and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I will, sir,’ but he did not go.
NIV.31:
“Which of the two did what his father wanted?”
“The first,” they answered.
Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God ahead of you.
NIV.32: For John came to you to show you the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes did. And even after you saw this, you did not repent and believe him.
NIV.33:
The Parable of the Tenants
“Listen to another parable: There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a winepress in it and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and went away on a journey.
NIV.34: When the harvest time approached, he sent his servants to the tenants to collect his fruit.
NIV.35: “The tenants seized his servants; they beat one, killed another, and stoned a third.
NIV.36: Then he sent other servants to them, more than the first time, and the tenants treated them the same way.
NIV.37: Last of all, he sent his son to them. ‘They will respect my son,’ he said.
NIV.38: “But when the tenants saw the son, they said to each other, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him and take his inheritance.’
NIV.39: So they took him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
NIV.40: “Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
NIV.41: “He will bring those wretches to a wretched end,” they replied, “and he will rent the vineyard to other tenants, who will give him his share of the crop at harvest time.”
NIV.42:
Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures:
“‘The stone the builders rejected
has become the capstone;
the Lord has done this,
and it is marvelous in our eyes’?
NIV.43: “Therefore I tell you that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people who will produce its fruit.
NIV.44: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed.”
NIV.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard Jesus’ parables, they knew he was talking about them.
NIV.46: They looked for a way to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowd because the people held that he was a prophet.

NRSVCE.1: When they had come near Jerusalem and had reached Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
NRSVCE.2: saying to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to me.
NRSVCE.3: If anyone says anything to you, just say this, ‘The Lord needs them.’ And he will send them immediately. ”
NRSVCE.4: This took place to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet, saying,
NRSVCE.5:
“Tell the daughter of Zion,
Look, your king is coming to you,
humble, and mounted on a donkey,
and on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
NRSVCE.6: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them;
NRSVCE.7: they brought the donkey and the colt, and put their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.
NRSVCE.8: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NRSVCE.9:
The crowds that went ahead of him and that followed were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David!
Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
NRSVCE.10: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in turmoil, asking, “Who is this?”
NRSVCE.11: The crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.”
NRSVCE.12: Then Jesus entered the temple and drove out all who were selling and buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
NRSVCE.13:
He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer’;
but you are making it a den of robbers.”
NRSVCE.14: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he cured them.
NRSVCE.15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the amazing things that he did, and heard the children crying out in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they became angry
NRSVCE.16:
and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes; have you never read, ‘Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babies
you have prepared praise for yourself’?”
NRSVCE.17: He left them, went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
NRSVCE.18: In the morning, when he returned to the city, he was hungry.
NRSVCE.19: And seeing a fig tree by the side of the road, he went to it and found nothing at all on it but leaves. Then he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” And the fig tree withered at once.
NRSVCE.20: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed, saying, “How did the fig tree wither at once?”
NRSVCE.21: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done.
NRSVCE.22: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.”
NRSVCE.23: When he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
NRSVCE.24: Jesus said to them, “I will also ask you one question; if you tell me the answer, then I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.
NRSVCE.25: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?” And they argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
NRSVCE.26: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we are afraid of the crowd; for all regard John as a prophet.”
NRSVCE.27: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
NRSVCE.28: “What do you think? A man had two sons; he went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
NRSVCE.29: He answered, ‘I will not’; but later he changed his mind and went.
NRSVCE.30: The father went to the second and said the same; and he answered, ‘I go, sir’; but he did not go.
NRSVCE.31: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are going into the kingdom of God ahead of you.
NRSVCE.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him; and even after you saw it, you did not change your minds and believe him.
NRSVCE.33: “Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a wine press in it, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
NRSVCE.34: When the harvest time had come, he sent his slaves to the tenants to collect his produce.
NRSVCE.35: But the tenants seized his slaves and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
NRSVCE.36: Again he sent other slaves, more than the first; and they treated them in the same way.
NRSVCE.37: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NRSVCE.38: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him and get his inheritance.’
NRSVCE.39: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
NRSVCE.40: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
NRSVCE.41: They said to him, “He will put those wretches to a miserable death, and lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the produce at the harvest time.”
NRSVCE.42:
Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the scriptures: ‘The stone that the builders rejected
has become the cornerstone;
this was the Lord’s doing,
and it is amazing in our eyes’?
NRSVCE.43: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people that produces the fruits of the kingdom.
NRSVCE.44: The one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
NRSVCE.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized that he was speaking about them.
NRSVCE.46: They wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, because they regarded him as a prophet.

NET.1: Now when they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
NET.2: telling them, “Go to the village ahead of you. Right away you will find a donkey tied there, and a colt with her. Untie them and bring them to me.
NET.3: If anyone says anything to you, you are to say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and he will send them at once.”
NET.4: This took place to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet:
NET.5: “Tell the people of Zion, ‘Look, your king is coming to you, unassuming and seated on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey.’”
NET.6: So the disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed them.
NET.7: They brought the donkey and the colt and placed their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.
NET.8: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road. Others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NET.9: The crowds that went ahead of him and those following kept shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
NET.10: As he entered Jerusalem the whole city was thrown into an uproar, saying, “Who is this?”
NET.11: And the crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth in Galilee.”
NET.12: Then Jesus entered the temple area and drove out all those who were selling and buying in the temple courts, and turned over the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves.
NET.13: And he said to them, “It is written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer,’ but you are turning it into a den of robbers!”
NET.14: The blind and lame came to him in the temple courts, and he healed them.
NET.15: But when the chief priests and the experts in the law saw the wonderful things he did and heard the children crying out in the temple courts, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they became indignant
NET.16: and said to him, “Do you hear what they are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes. Have you never read, ‘Out of the mouths of children and nursing infants you have prepared praise for yourself’?”
NET.17: And leaving them, he went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
NET.18: Now early in the morning, as he returned to the city, he was hungry.
NET.19: After noticing a fig tree by the road he went to it, but found nothing on it except leaves. He said to it, “Never again will there be fruit from you!” And the fig tree withered at once.
NET.20: When the disciples saw it they were amazed, saying, “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?”
NET.21: Jesus answered them, “I tell you the truth, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will happen.
NET.22: And whatever you ask in prayer, if you believe, you will receive.”
NET.23: Now after Jesus entered the temple courts, the chief priests and elders of the people came up to him as he was teaching and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
NET.24: Jesus answered them, “I will also ask you one question. If you answer me then I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.
NET.25: Where did John’s baptism come from? From heaven or from people?” They discussed this among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Then why did you not believe him?’
NET.26: But if we say, ‘From people,’ we fear the crowd, for they all consider John to be a prophet.”
NET.27: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Then he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
NET.28: “What do you think? A man had two sons. He went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
NET.29: The boy answered, ‘I will not.’ But later he had a change of heart and went.
NET.30: The father went to the other son and said the same thing. This boy answered, ‘I will, sir,’ but did not go.
NET.31: Which of the two did his father’s will?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, tax collectors and prostitutes will go ahead of you into the kingdom of God!
NET.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him. But the tax collectors and prostitutes did believe. Although you saw this, you did not later change your minds and believe him.
NET.33: “Listen to another parable: There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a pit for its winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenant farmers and went on a journey.
NET.34: When the harvest time was near, he sent his slaves to the tenants to collect his portion of the crop.
NET.35: But the tenants seized his slaves, beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
NET.36: Again he sent other slaves, more than the first, and they treated them the same way.
NET.37: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NET.38: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him and get his inheritance!’
NET.39: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
NET.40: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
NET.41: They said to him, “He will utterly destroy those evil men! Then he will lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him his portion at the harvest.”
NET.42: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the scriptures: ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This is from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?
NET.43: For this reason I tell you that the kingdom of God will be taken from you and given to a people who will produce its fruit.
NET.44: The one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, and the one on whom it falls will be crushed.”
NET.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized that he was speaking about them.
NET.46: They wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowds, because the crowds regarded him as a prophet.
